summary: Broad GR/stage decomp PR that factored a repeated stage-GObj callback setup pattern into the inline helper Ground_SetupStageCallbacks, swept many stage loaders to use it, and added matching work for Push On plus callback/data tables and setup functions in Corneria, Home Run, Old Kongo, and Old Pupupu. decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code at 58.52% (+0.04%, +1740 bytes), with 8 new grpushon matches and 14 additional improvements.
what_changed: Added static inline Ground_SetupStageCallbacks(HSD_GObj*, StageCallbacks*) in src/melee/gr/inlines.h. The helper clears Ground x8_callback/xC_callback, sets the GX link to grDisplay_801C5DB0, installs callback3 into x1C_callback, invokes callback0, and registers callback2 as a proc.

Replaced the same hand-written setup sequence with Ground_SetupStageCallbacks across many gr stage files, including figure, target-test, shrine, story, garden, fourside, test, IceMt, Pura, and many grt* modules.

Decompiled major parts of src/melee/gr/grpushon.c: StageCallbacks grPushOn_803E7AC8, StageData grPushOn_803E7B10, grPushOn_802183E4, grPushOn_802184CC, grPushOn_802185A0, grPushOn_802186C8, grPushOn_802187A8, grPushOn_80218ED4, grPushOn_80218FC0, and grPushOn_80219458.
Added Push On state typing through struct grPushOn_GroundVars and the Ground union member u.pushon, covering stored light objects, original lobj flags, count, point_light, and spot_light.

Added HSD_LObjSetNext in src/sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.h with HSD_ASSERT(0x136, lobj), then reused it in lb_00F9.c, mnstagesel.c, and new Push On light-list code instead of open-coding lobj->next assignments/assertions.
Added StageCallbacks data tables such as grCn_803E1D8C[19], grHr_803E8140[11], grOk_803E658C[4], and grOp_803E6688[9], improving .data matching for multiple stages.
Implemented or improved stage object setup functions grCorneria_801DD534, grHomeRun_8021C82C, grOldKongo_8020F52C, and grIceMt_801F71E8 by using typed StageCallbacks and the shared setup helper.

Implemented grHomeRun_8021C754 initialization logic, including Ground_801C49F8 storage, stage_info flag writes, creation/setup calls for gobj ids 1 and 0xA, and camera/blast-zone based Ground offset calculations.

Tightened several prototypes in headers, including grPushOn_802183E4 returning HSD_GObj*, grPushOn_80218ED4/grPushOn_80218FC0 returning HSD_LObj*, fn_802190A0 taking an extra f32, grPushOn_802190D0 taking HSD_GObj*, grHomeRun_8021C82C returning HSD_GObj*, and grHomeRun_8021EDD4 taking int.
smart_moves: The PR recognized that many stage loader functions were the same callback-setup idiom and centralized it as a static inline; this kept source deduplication while preserving callsite-shaped code for matching.
Doing the helper conversion broadly was validated by review: ribbanya said the scope was not a problem and was preferred over multiple smaller PRs.

Adding StageCallbacks tables before or alongside setup functions unlocked both code and .data progress, as shown by improvements in grcorneria .data, grhomerun .data, grpushon .data, and groldkongo .data.
The HSD_LObjSetNext helper preserved the previous assertion semantics and source line evidence through HSD_ASSERT(0x136, lobj), making it reusable without losing the expected assert behavior.
Typing Push On's Ground union storage made later code readable and matchable: lobj arrays, flags, count, point_light, and spot_light replaced opaque padding/raw offsets.
The new Push On light helpers use existing baselib primitives, HSD_LObjLoadDesc, HSD_LObjSetNext, HSD_LObjSetPosition, and HSD_LObjSetFlags, rather than manually manipulating every light field.
decomp_lessons: For gr stage setup functions, look for the recurring sequence Ground_GetStageGObj -> clear Ground callbacks -> GObj_SetupGXLink -> optional callback3/callback0/callback2. This PR codifies that as Ground_SetupStageCallbacks.
Stage callback tables are high-value decomp targets: once the StageCallbacks arrays and flag constants are named, multiple nearby setup functions often collapse to the same small pattern.
Prefer static inline helpers for repeated low-level patterns when a real shared function would change codegen; this is especially useful in decomp source cleanups that must still match.
Header/type work can be matching work: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with HSD_GObj*, HSD_LObj*, DynamicsDesc*, and typed callback signatures made the decompiled functions express the real ABI.
Assert wrappers should preserve line numbers and macro behavior. HSD_LObjSetNext retained the lobj.h 0x136 assertion while enabling concise source at callsites.

OSReport strings and numeric line arguments matter for matching. This PR kept explicit file names/line constants in some places and changed others to __FILE__ only where the diff evidence indicated it matched.
Per-stage Ground union overlays should be introduced when a stage repeatedly accesses the same gp+C4 region; grPushOn_GroundVars is the model from this PR.
naming_conventions: Stage callback data follows gr + abbreviated stage prefix + address: grCn_803E1D8C, grHr_803E8140, grOk_803E658C, grOp_803E6688, grPushOn_803E7AC8.
StageData symbols use the stage prefix and address, e.g. grPushOn_803E7B10.
Unknown or not-yet-semantic functions retained address-based names such as grPushOn_80218ED4, grHomeRun_8021C82C, and grCorneria_801DD534.
The shared helper uses the Ground_ prefix because it operates on Ground-owned stage GObj state: Ground_SetupStageCallbacks.
Local StageCallbacks pointers were normalized toward the descriptive name callbacks instead of shorter cb in several touched functions.
Per-stage Ground state struct names use the full stage name plus _GroundVars, e.g. grPushOn_GroundVars, with a lower-case union member pushon.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a static inline helper in gr/inlines.h to avoid introducing a runtime call while removing duplicated source blocks.
Wrapped grCorneria_801DD534 with #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop, indicating inlining control was needed for matching that function.
Used PAD_STACK in new Push On functions, including PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(12), and PAD_STACK(16), to satisfy stack-layout/codegen requirements.
Preserved exact callback setup order: set x8/xC null, setup GX link, assign callback3, call callback0, then setup callback2 proc.
Preserved StageCallbacks flag constants such as 0x80000000, 0x40000000, 0x20000000, and 0xC0000000 in newly materialized tables.
Used explicit OSReport file/line constants where shown in the diff, such as grpushon.c line 222 and grhomerun.c 0x131, because those literals affect generated data/code.
The Push On lobj traversal used null-preserving next expressions and HSD_LObjSetNext rather than raw next writes, matching existing baselib assertion patterns.
Camera and blast-zone calculations in grPushOn_802184CC and grHomeRun_8021C754 preserved literal float factors 0.5f and 3.0f in the source.
review_feedback: Author note: the PR was described as 'kinda unfocused'; most of it was said to be commit 094aabc2e93b5445ce87971840956a3ef3be23ec, factoring a common pattern into a helper.
decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code 58.52% (+0.04%, +1740 bytes).

decomp-dev bot listed 8 new 100% grpushon matches: grPushOn_80218ED4, grPushOn_802183E4, grPushOn_80218FC0, grPushOn_802186C8, grPushOn_802187A8, grPushOn_802185A0, grPushOn_80219458, and grPushOn_802184CC.

decomp-dev bot listed 14 improvements, including grCorneria_801DD534 to 99.95%, grHomeRun_8021C82C to 99.97%, grOldKongo_8020F52C to 94.88%, grHomeRun_8021C754 to 96.90%, and grIceMt_801F71E8 to 99.95%.
decomp-dev bot reported one regression: main/melee/gr/grpura grPura_802125F0, -48 bytes, 75.26% to 72.51%.
Reviewer ribbanya approved and wrote: 'The scope of this PR is not a problem; in fact, it is preferred over multiple smaller ones.'
No line review comments were present in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining gr stage setup functions that still open-code the Ground_SetupStageCallbacks pattern.
Investigate whether the reported grPura_802125F0 regression from this PR was later fixed.
Continue Push On work around fn_802192A4 and any still-unmatched grPushOn functions near grPushOn_802190D0.
Search for other raw lobj->next writes that can safely use HSD_LObjSetNext while preserving assertion behavior.
Use the StageCallbacks table approach on other stages with poor .data matching.
Audit OSReport file/line literal choices in stage setup functions when future refactors affect string/data matching.
